summary:
The presence of two palatal roots is common in maxillary first molars and generally ranges between approximately 2% to 10%.

Key Clinical Points Summary for Question: Frequency range for having two palatal roots in a maxillary first molar is around 2%-10%, based on usual clinical observations rather than exact statistics presented here. To provide precise percentages requires further research or detailed study findings not included above. Agent2 concluded with an educated estimate within this known variability.
